---
subtitle: "Establishing the Empirical Baseline for AI-SDG Analysis"
abstract: |
  This chapter sets the empirical baseline for the book by summarizing the Technology Readiness Level (TRL) scaffold used throughout Parts I--III and the multi-database corpus (Scopus, Web of Science, Lens, EPO/USPTO patents) used for scientometric and patent horizon scanning.
keywords:
  - scientometrics
  - bibliometrics
  - patent landscaping
  - TRL
  - multi-database triangulation
---

# Scientometric & Patent Horizon {#sec-scientometric-horizon}

Scientometric and patent horizon scanning triangulates scholarly publications, patent filings, and institutional knowledge outputs into a single, de-duplicated corpus. That corpus is the empirical baseline every later chapter builds on: Part I turns it into technology trajectories, Part II translates those trajectories into design canvases, and Part III turns the design outputs into standards and compliance artefacts.

No single database offers full coverage on its own, so this chapter treats multi-database triangulation, patent-based technology mining, and a shared Technology Readiness Level (TRL) rubric as the three prerequisites for everything that follows.

## Multi-Database Ingestion
- Comparative coverage of Scopus, Web of Science, and Lens for SDG tracking; strengths/blind spots of each source.
- Data harvesting and taxonomy alignment against the 17 SDGs (cross-ref @sec-sdg-target-mapping).
- Deduplication and record-linkage across scholarly and patent corpora.

## Technology Readiness Level (TRL) Assessment Framework
- TRL 1--9 scoring rubric adapted for AI-enabled hardware/software.
- Worked example: applying the rubric to a semiconductor sub-domain.

## Patent Landscape Analysis & Technology Mining
- The International Patent Classification (IPC) and Cooperative Patent Classification (CPC) classification, citation velocity, key-assignee mapping.
- Co-patenting networks as an early signal of cross-border collaboration.

## Institutional Knowledge Landscapes {#sec-institutional-baseline}
- Comparative mapping of UNU, OECD, and ADB published knowledge bases as a *baseline corpus*, distinct from the commercial databases above.
- Sets up the knowledge-to-policy nexus revisited in @sec-sdg-target-mapping.

::: {.callout-tip title="Bridge to Chapter 2"}
Chapter 2 turns this raw, triangulated corpus into technology trajectories using AI-assisted content analysis, natural language processing (NLP), and prompt priming, in an AI-Powered Bibliometrics Catalogue.
:::
